Notice Of Motion To Compel Compliance, Further Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANTS GEORGE LONGORIA and RETAIL BRAND ALLIANCE, INC DBA BROOKS BROTHERS' Motion To Compel Compliance, Further Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions is granted. As to Form Interrogatory Nos. 212.4, 212.5, 213.1, and 213.2, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. As the responses state that discovery is continuing, they are inadequate. The reservation of a right to amend is stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further. As to the Requests For Production Nos. 5, 10, 12, 13, 19, 20, 21, 22, 24, 39, and 40, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken with the exception of assertions of privilege. The court does not make a finding as to whether additional documents exist. However, Plaintiff is ordered to make reasonable and diligent search for responsive documents and shall identify with particularity any documents which may be privileged. Plaintiff's further response and production must verify that all responsive documents have been produced.
The same rulings are made as to Nos. 25 and 38. Additionally, the court finds that the right to privacy has been waived as it relates to any allegation of emotional distress and medical treatment arising out Plaintiff's allegations. The motion is granted as to No. 29 as there is no evidence to support a finding that they constituted communications with counsel and were prepared in anticipation of litigation. Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. The court finds that the objections were made without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $2,200, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.
